meaning: adv.命令式地,有权威地,可信地
examples: A judge should comport himself authoritatively [with dignity].
"Look, I'm having a stroke," Maloney told the doctor authoritatively.
The hallmark of law is the obligation to act in line with norms authoritatively determined.
The point is though that by late today I imagine this story will be quoted authoritatively across the world.
Chief executives trust the personal opinions of their customers, who voluntarily and authoritatively choose what to buy with their own money.
